page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with 96 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, this instance features 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is dedicated, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Cache specifications include 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX support.

This instance does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with the instance. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for operating systems and data, which can impact I/O latency compared to local NVMe options.

In benchmark evaluations, the E96as_v5 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 129540.53 in stress-ng multi-core tests, which falls in the top 10 percent. Geekbench multi-core performance is strong at 18509.0, alongside strong results in software compilation and ray tracing. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the average category. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.0 GB per vCPU provides a dense resource profile for memory-intensive workloads without the premium cost of GPU-accelerated hardware.

Given the high memory capacity and strong multi-core processing capabilities, this server is suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise application servers, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E96as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores, it utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score in the top 10 percent and strong Geekbench multi-core results. Conversely, single-core and text processing benchmarks remain in the average tier. This high memory-to-vCPU density makes the instance cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads. It is suited for large databases, in-memory analytics, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v5 is an EASv5 VM with 96 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and AMD EPYC processing for demanding database and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core performance ranking in the top 10 percent of stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
